intent.goal_statement: Add batch segment calibration to the narrator-tts worker so the optimal GPU batch size is determined empirically rather than through theoretical VRAM estimates.
intent.motivation: The current VRAM-based batch size estimate is too conservative on consumer GPUs, returning batch_size=1 when 6 segments would fit. Empirical calibration finds the true maximum.
intent.success_definition: Workers automatically determine and cache the optimal batch size per model+GPU combination without manual configuration.
intent.scope_context: narrator-tts CLI application, Rust codebase, tch backend with CUDA support

assumptions: The tch backend exposes query_cuda_memory() returning (free_bytes, total_bytes) on Windows
The qbench pipeline already accepts multiple --texts arguments via Vec<String>
GPU OOM produces a detectable exit code or stderr pattern from the TTS backend

approach.overview: Add a calibration probe phase at worker startup that measures actual VRAM per segment via binary search, caches the result per model+GPU, and uses the calibrated batch size for all subsequent dispatch.
approach.rationale: Empirical measurement is more accurate than theoretical VRAM estimates. Binary search converges in log2(N) probes. Caching avoids repeated calibration.

alternatives_considered.rejection_reason: Requires user experimentation to find the right value; different for every GPU+model combination.

alternatives_considered.approach: Fix the theoretical VRAM estimate formula
alternatives_considered.rejection_reason: The formula would need to account for CUDA fragmentation, driver overhead, and model-specific memory patterns. Empirical measurement is simpler and more robust.

steps.title: Create batch calibration module
steps.description: Implement a BatchCalibrator struct in src/backends/tch_tts.rs that probes the GPU with synthetic segments to find the maximum stable batch size. Uses binary search between batch_size=1 and the conservative estimate from recommended_max_batch_size() * 8.
steps.rationale: Isolating calibration logic in a dedicated module keeps the worker and backend clean.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Calibrator returns a usize >= 1. Binary search terminates within log2(max_probe) iterations.

steps.title: Implement batch size cache
steps.description: Create a JSON cache file at ~/.narrator/batch_cache.json keyed by model path hash + GPU device ID + available VRAM. Check cache before calibration; skip if hit. Add --recalibrate CLI flag to force re-probe.
steps.rationale: Avoids running calibration on every TTS session. Cache invalidates naturally on model or GPU changes.
steps.inputs: Calibrated batch size, model path, GPU device info
steps.acceptance_criteria: Cache read returns correct batch size on second run without probe. --recalibrate forces re-probe.

steps.title: Extend WorkAssignment for multi-segment batches
steps.description: Add a segments: Vec<String> field to WorkAssignment alongside the existing single text field. Use serde default for backward compatibility with legacy IPC messages.
steps.rationale: The IPC protocol must carry multiple segments per assignment.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Old single-segment IPC messages deserialize correctly. New messages with segments field work.

steps.title: Implement batch grouping in orchestrator
steps.description: Modify the orchestrator dispatch loop to collect up to N pending segments and package them into a single batched WorkAssignment. N comes from the calibrated batch size.
steps.rationale: This is the core throughput improvement — batching segments before sending to the worker.
steps.outputs: Batched WorkAssignments sent to worker pool
steps.acceptance_criteria: With batch_size=1, behavior is identical to current. With batch_size>1, multiple segments sent per assignment.

steps.title: Update worker to pass multiple --texts
steps.description: Modify run_chunk_threaded() to detect multi-segment WorkAssignment and pass each segment as a separate --texts argument to qbench.
steps.rationale: qbench already accepts multiple texts; the worker just needs to construct the right CLI invocation.
steps.outputs: Multiple WAV files in segment order
steps.acceptance_criteria: Worker invokes qbench once with all --texts. WAV files returned in correct order.

steps.title: Implement OOM fallback retry
steps.description: When a batch fails with OOM, retry with half the batch size. Continue halving until success or batch_size=1.
steps.rationale: Runtime VRAM conditions may differ from calibration time. Fallback prevents hard failures.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Batch of size N that OOMs retries at N/2, N/4, etc. Successful size reported to orchestrator.

steps.title: Add batch tests and verify backward compatibility
steps.description: Run existing test suite. Add unit tests for: calibration module, cache read/write, batch grouping, multi-segment serialization, OOM fallback. Verify default behavior unchanged.
steps.rationale: No regressions and new code paths covered.
steps.acceptance_criteria: All existing tests pass. New tests cover batch calibration, cache, grouping, fallback.

constraints.description: Calibration must not corrupt the loaded model state. Probes must be non-destructive.
constraints.validation_method: Model generates correctly after calibration phase completes.

constraints.description: Calibration overhead must be under 30 seconds for any GPU.
constraints.validation_method: Time calibration probes on 12.3 GB GPU target.

success_criteria.description: --chunk-batch-size defaults to auto with numeric override available
Calibration finds max stable batch size within log2(probe_range) iterations
Batch size cached per model+GPU combo, skip calibration on cache hit
OOM triggers automatic fallback to smaller batch

risks.title: Calibration segments produce poor audio that pollutes the output
risks.description: Synthetic probe segments could generate audible artifacts if not discarded.
risks.mitigation: Calibration output is discarded. Probes use minimal synthetic text.
risks.contingency: Isolate calibration in a temp directory; delete all artifacts on completion.

risks.title: OOM detection is unreliable across CUDA/tch versions
risks.description: Different CUDA versions may report OOM differently, causing fallback to miss or false-trigger.
risks.mitigation: Check both exit code and stderr patterns. Default to batch_size=1 if OOM detection is ambiguous.
risks.contingency: Log OOM detection events for post-run analysis. Manual --chunk-batch-size override as escape hatch.

step_overrides.rationale: Pure implementation, no design decisions needed.

revision_history.author: planclave
revision_history.summary: Initial plan: batch calibration with binary search, cache, multi-segment dispatch, OOM fallback.
